title: Bridge Essay: Effects of slow-paced breathing and humming breathing on heart rate variability

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Breath/Soul** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Breath and soul — pranayama, breath-brain coupling, respiratory-brain interaction, cardiorespiratory entrainment, vagal pathways of consciousness.

**Effects of slow-paced breathing and humming breathing on heart rate variability and affect: apilot investigation.** — Physiol Behav. PMID: 40482984. DOI: 10.1016/j.physbeh.2025.114972. This study aimed to evaluate the effects of slow-paced breathing (SPB) and humming breathing on heart rate variability (HRV) and affect. The study included 16 healthy adults. HRV was measured for five min under three conditions: rest, SPB (5-second inhale, 5 sexhale; administered using the EliteHRV application), and humming breathing (Bhramari Pranayama). The Visual Analogue Scale (VAS) assessed anxiety, stress, relaxation, and confidence across baseline, rest, SPB, and humming breathing condit...

notes: Auto-acquired from PubMed/MEDLINE on 2026-07-12. Phase 2 (Breath/Soul). Evaluation: Excellent (score=12.0)
